description: Scanning electron micrographs showing manganese oxide threads on the surfaces of (a) Fe-20Mn-8Al and (b) Fe-30Mn-8Al after 24 hr oxidation at 800°C in pure oxygen at 200 torr. Subfigure (a) displays fine, wormlike manganese oxide threads distributed across the oxidized surface, which the authors associate with a relatively high ferrite content, noting that such threads grew above grain boundaries and at distinct orientations on grain surfaces. Subfigure (b) exhibits more prominent wormlike oxide threads together with bulky manganese-rich oxides; the authors report that these morphologies appeared to grow through the alumina scale and are characteristic of alloys containing higher proportions of austenite. Both micrographs reveal thread-like oxide formations directly linked to substrate structure. The comparison supports the core conclusion that austenite promotes the breakdown of preexisting protective alumina scales and stimulates the growth of manganese-rich oxide threads, whereas optimal oxidation resistance occurs in fully ferritic alloys within the Fe–(5–10)% Mn–(6–10)% Al composition range that develop continuous alumina scales.
